$comment: Rules for command-guard.js. Fragments are normalised (wrappers/absolute-paths/base64/here-docs/`-c` payloads stripped or decoded) BEFORE matching, so these regexes see the real verb even when the model tries `env git`, `/usr/bin/git`, `$(which git)`, `base64 -d | bash`, `bash -c '...'`, `python3 -c '...'`, etc. Patterns are case-insensitive. action: deny (hard block) | ask (hard block asking for explicit human approval) | warn (log + pass through). A fragment matches a rule if ANY `patterns` regex hits AND NO `unless` regex hits. Tune freely — this is add-only, not a security boundary; real enforcement belongs in an OS sandbox (bubblewrap/Landlock).

rules.message: Recursive/force delete targeting /, ~, $HOME, /etc, /usr, /var, /boot or a wildcard at filesystem root is blocked outright.
No rm command runs unconfirmed: every file/directory deletion needs explicit human approval. (Deletion of / ~ $HOME or system paths is blocked outright by rm-rf-dangerous-target.)
Force-push rewrites remote history. Use --force-with-lease and confirm the target branch.
`git reset --hard` discards uncommitted work. Confirm before running.
`git clean -f`/-fdx wipes untracked files (a classic accidental data-loss). Blocked.
`git checkout .` / `git restore .` discards all local changes. Confirm before running.
History-rewriting git ops (filter-branch/filter-repo/rebase -i, reflog expire, gc --prune=now) can destroy commits. Confirm.
Piping a downloaded script straight into a shell (curl|bash / wget|sh) is blocked. Download, review, then run.
Raw `dd` writes to a device path are blocked (can destroy disks).
Redirecting output onto a /dev/ device or into /sys is blocked.
Recursive world-writable chmod on a system/home path is dangerous. Confirm scope.
Reading private keys / credentials via a shell command is blocked (use is almost never legitimate for the agent).
Command tries to disable/erase shell history — flagged for visibility.
